title: Train a censored time-to-next-pit specialist and blend it AUC-safely
summary: Each row is naturally a time-to-event state.
rationale: Each row is naturally a time-to-event state. Survival models explicitly handle censored final stints, and recent public F1 pit-stop work finds temporal models useful. For leaderboard ROC-AUC, blend this specialist with the best tabular model using rank-preserving methods; scikit-learn notes isotonic can introduce ties and change AUC.
implementation_hint: Convert each driver-race sequence into time-until-next-pit with right-censoring at race end, fit an XGBoost `survival:aft` or time-varying hazard model, and use next-lap hazard/probability as a meta-feature or OOF blend with the base GBM. Prefer rank averaging or sigmoid-only calibration over isotonic when optimizing AUC.
expected_effect: Adds an event-time inductive bias that pure row-wise classifiers lack and may improve public robustness when exact stop timing drives rank.
risk: More complex CV plumbing; survival objectives optimize likelihood, so the specialist may help mainly through ensembling rather than standalone CV.
